---
title: "TSLA Advanced Charting"
format:
  html:
    theme: [cosmo, theme.scss]
    page-layout: full
    self-contained: true
---

<head>
  <meta name="description" content="Tesla stock historical data interactive charting webpage.">
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@400;600&family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
</head>

<body>

::: {.column-screen}
<div id="chart-container" class="chart-wrapper">
<div class="chart-toolbar">
<div class="toolbar-left">
<span class="ticker-badge">TSLA</span>
<span class="ticker-title">Tesla, Inc.</span>
<span class="divider">|</span>
<div class="indicator-buttons">
<button class="tool-btn active" id="btn-sma" onclick="toggleIndicators('sma')">SMA Lines</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-bb" onclick="toggleIndicators('bb')">Bollinger Bands</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-all" onclick="toggleIndicators('all')">Show All</button>
<button class="tool-btn" id="btn-clear" onclick="toggleIndicators('clear')">Price Only</button>
</div>
</div>
<button id="fullscreen-btn" onclick="toggleFullscreen()">⛶ Full Screen</button>
</div>

```{python}
#| label: advanced-chart
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# Load the prepared dataset
df = pd.read_csv("../downloads/tsla_enriched.csv")
df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['date'])

# Create subplots: Price (row 1) and Volume (row 2)
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1, 
    shared_xaxes=True, 
    vertical_spacing=0.03,
    subplot_titles=('Price & Technical Indicators', 'Volume'),
    row_width=[0.2, 0.8]  # Row 2 (Volume) is 20%, Row 1 (Price) is 80%
)

# Determine candle colors (green if Close >= Open, else red)
colors = ['#26a69a' if row['close'] >= row['open'] else '#ef5350' for _, row in df.iterrows()]

# 1. Add Candlestick trace (Row 1)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Candlestick(
        x=df['date'],
        open=df['open'],
        high=df['high'],
        low=df['low'],
        close=df['close'],
        name='TSLA Price',
        increasing_line_color='#26a69a', 
        decreasing_line_color='#ef5350',
        increasing_fillcolor='#26a69a',
        decreasing_fillcolor='#ef5350',
        showlegend=True
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# 2. Add SMA Lines (Row 1)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df['date'], y=df['sma_20'],
        line=dict(color='#2962ff', width=1.5),
        name='SMA 20',
        visible=True
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df['date'], y=df['sma_50'],
        line=dict(color='#ff9800', width=1.5),
        name='SMA 50',
        visible=True
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df['date'], y=df['sma_200'],
        line=dict(color='#e040fb', width=1.5),
        name='SMA 200',
        visible=True
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# 3. Add Bollinger Bands (Row 1)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df['date'], y=df['bb_upper'],
        line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'),
        name='BB Upper',
        visible=False
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=df['date'], y=df['bb_lower'],
        line=dict(color='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.5)', width=1, dash='dash'),
        fill='tonexty',  # Shaded region between Upper and Lower
        fillcolor='rgba(173, 20, 87, 0.05)',
        name='BB Lower',
        visible=False
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# 4. Add Volume bars (Row 2)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=df['date'], y=df['volume'],
        name='Volume',
        marker=dict(color=colors, line=dict(width=0)),
        opacity=1.0,
        showlegend=False
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

# Layout and custom dark theme modifications
fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_dark',
    paper_bgcolor='#0c101b',  # Deep slate-blue charting background
    plot_bgcolor='#0c101b',
    autosize=True,
    dragmode='pan',  # Make PAN the default drag mode
    margin=dict(t=45, b=40, l=60, r=40),  # Subdued top margin since title is moved to HTML toolbar
    xaxis=dict(
        rangeslider=dict(visible=False),  # Hide range slider to keep subplots clean
        rangeselector=dict(
            buttons=list([
                dict(count=1, label="1M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=3, label="3M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=6, label="6M", step="month", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=1, label="1Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                dict(step="all", label="MAX")
            ]),
            bgcolor='#141824',
            activecolor='#2962ff',
            font=dict(color='#d1d4dc', size=11),
            bordercolor='#202637',
            borderwidth=1
        )
    ),
    yaxis=dict(
        gridcolor='#202637',
        zerolinecolor='#202637',
        tickprefix='$',
        title='Price (USD)',
        fixedrange=True  # Lock Y-axis from zooming/panning
    ),
    yaxis2=dict(
        gridcolor='#202637',
        zerolinecolor='#202637',
        title='Volume',
        fixedrange=True  # Lock Y-axis from zooming/panning
    ),
    legend=dict(
        orientation='h',
        yanchor='bottom',
        y=1.02,
        xanchor='right',
        x=1,
        bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)'
    )
)

# Constrain the X-axis panning and zooming to the available data range
fig.update_xaxes(
    minallowed='2010-06-15',
    maxallowed='2026-07-05'
)

# Show figure with Scroll Zoom enabled
fig.show(config={'scrollZoom': True})
```

</div>

<script>
function toggleFullscreen() {
  var el = document.getElementById('chart-container');
  el.classList.toggle('fullscreen');
  var btn = document.getElementById('fullscreen-btn');
  if (el.classList.contains('fullscreen')) {
    btn.innerHTML = "🗖 Exit Full Screen";
  } else {
    btn.innerHTML = "⛶ Full Screen";
  }
  // Let Plotly adjust to new size (after transition)
  setTimeout(function() {
    window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize'));
  }, 150);
}

function toggleIndicators(type) {
  var gd = document.querySelector('.plotly-graph-div');
  if (!gd) return;
  
  // Update button active state
  var buttons = document.querySelectorAll('.indicator-buttons .tool-btn');
  buttons.forEach(function(btn) {
    btn.classList.remove('active');
  });
  
  var activeBtn = document.getElementById('btn-' + type);
  if (activeBtn) {
    activeBtn.classList.add('active');
  }
  
  if (type === 'sma') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: true }, [1, 2, 3]);
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: false }, [4, 5]);
  } else if (type === 'bb') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: false }, [1, 2, 3]);
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: true }, [4, 5]);
  } else if (type === 'all') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: true }, [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
  } else if (type === 'clear') {
    Plotly.restyle(gd, { visible: false }, [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]);
  }
}
</script>
:::

</body>
